title: The Celebrated Jumping Frog of Calaveras County
titleZh: 卡拉维拉斯县闻名的跳蛙

q.en: Who narrates the outer (frame) story of the tale?
q.zh: 这个故事外层（框架）的叙述者是谁？

options.en: An unnamed narrator who visits Simon Wheeler at a friend's request
options.zh: 一位无名叙述者，应朋友之托去拜访西蒙·惠勒

options.en: Simon Wheeler himself
Jim Smiley
Mark Twain

explain.en: 'In compliance with the request of a friend of mine... I called on good-natured, garrulous old Simon Wheeler,' establishing the unnamed narrator who frames Wheeler's tale.
explain.zh: 开头写道'应一位朋友的请求……我去拜访了和善而喋喋不休的老西蒙·惠勒'，确立了这位为惠勒故事做框架的无名叙述者。

q.en: What trick does the stranger play on Jim Smiley's frog, Dan'l Webster?
q.zh: 那个陌生人对吉姆·斯迈利的青蛙“丹尼尔·韦伯斯特“耍了什么花招？

options.en: He fills the frog with quail shot so it cannot jump
options.zh: 他把鹌鹑弹丸塞进青蛙体内，使它无法起跳

options.en: He teaches the frog to fly
He swaps it for a toy frog
options.zh: 他用玩具青蛙把它调了包

options.en: He feeds it poison

explain.en: The stranger 'pried his mouth open and took a teaspoon and filled him full of quail shot—filled him pretty near up to his chin,' so Dan'l 'couldn't budge.'
explain.zh: 陌生人'撬开它的嘴，用茶匙把它塞满鹌鹑弹丸——几乎塞到下巴'，于是丹尼尔'动弹不得'。

q.en: What is distinctive about Simon Wheeler's manner of telling the story?
q.zh: 西蒙·惠勒讲述故事的方式有什么特别之处？

options.en: He speaks in a monotonous, earnest, never-changing tone, unaware his story is funny
options.zh: 他用单调、认真、一成不变的语调讲述，并未察觉自己的故事很滑稽

options.en: He laughs loudly at his own jokes
options.zh: 他为自己讲的笑话放声大笑

options.en: He speaks in formal, educated language
options.zh: 他用正式、有教养的语言讲述

options.en: He refuses to tell the story

explain.en: 'He never smiled, he never frowned, he never changed his voice... there ran a vein of impressive earnestness and sincerity, which showed me plainly that... he regarded it as a really important matter.'
explain.zh: '他从不微笑，从不皱眉，从不改变语调……字里行间透着令人印象深刻的认真与真诚，这清楚地向我表明……他视之为一件真正重要的事。'

q.en: Why does Twain give the frog and the dog names like 'Dan'l Webster' and 'Andrew Jackson'?
q.zh: 吐温为什么给青蛙和狗起“丹尼尔·韦伯斯特“和“安德鲁·杰克逊“这样的名字？

options.en: To add humorous, ironic grandeur by naming animals after famous Americans
options.zh: 通过以著名美国人的名字为动物命名，增添幽默而讽刺的“宏大感“

options.en: Because they were real historical figures
options.zh: 因为它们是真实的历史人物

options.en: To confuse the reader
Because the author ran out of names
options.zh: 因为作者想不出别的名字

explain.en: The book's Social Studies note invites research into 'Andrew Jackson or Daniel Webster' and 'the reason Twain used his name in the story' — a comic device of granting famous names to a frog and a bulldog.
explain.zh: 书中的社会研究栏目要求研究'安德鲁·杰克逊或丹尼尔·韦伯斯特'以及'吐温在故事中使用其名字的原因'——这是一种将名人名字赋予青蛙和斗牛犬的喜剧手法。

q.en: What does the stranger's repeated line — 'I don't see no points about that frog that's any better'n any other frog' — reveal about his character?
q.zh: 陌生人反复说的那句话——“我看不出那只青蛙比别的青蛙强在哪儿“——揭示了他的什么性格？

options.en: He is cunning and dishonest, pretending ignorance while having cheated Smiley
options.zh: 他狡猾而不诚实，在骗了斯迈利的同时装作一无所知

options.en: He is genuinely impressed by the frog
options.zh: 他真的被那只青蛙折服了

options.en: He is Simon Wheeler in disguise
options.zh: 他是伪装起来的西蒙·惠勒

options.en: He wants to buy the frog

explain.en: He says this line BEFORE cheating (to downplay the frog) and AGAIN as he leaves with the money — showing he feigned indifference while knowingly deceiving Smiley.
explain.zh: 他在作弊前（贬低青蛙）和拿着钱离开时（语句一模一样）都说了这句话——表明他佯装漠然，实则蓄意欺骗斯迈利。

q.en: What theme does Twain explore through Jim Smiley's endless betting?
q.zh: 通过吉姆·斯迈利没完没了的打赌，吐温探讨了什么主题？

options.en: Human gullibility and the humorous folly of always needing to make a bet
options.zh: 人性的轻信，以及总想打赌这种滑稽的愚蠢

options.en: The importance of saving money
The value of hard work
The danger of traveling

explain.en: Smiley 'was the curiousest man about always betting on anything that turned up'; the Focus Question asks how the author uses humor to address 'honesty, dishonesty, and cunning.'
explain.zh: 斯迈利'对凡是出现的事都爱打赌，奇得没法说'；焦点问题问作者如何用幽默处理'诚实、不诚实与狡黠'。

q.en: What happens to Jim Smiley's bull-pup Andrew Jackson in his final fight?
q.zh: 吉姆·斯迈利的斗牛犬安德鲁·杰克逊在最后一场打斗中发生了什么？

options.en: He loses because the opposing dog has no hind legs to grab, and he dies of a broken heart
options.zh: 他输了，因为对手没有后腿可抓，最终心碎而死

options.en: He wins the championship
He runs away
He becomes a racing dog

explain.en: The other dog 'didn't have no hind legs, because they'd been sawed off.' Andrew Jackson 'looked sorter discouraged-like, and didn't try no more... and then he limped off a piece and laid down and died.'
explain.zh: 另一只狗'没有后腿，因为被锯掉了'。安德鲁·杰克逊'显得有些泄气，不再挣扎……一瘸一拐地走开，躺下死了'。

q.en: How much money does the stranger win from Smiley?
q.zh: 陌生人从斯迈利那里赢走了多少钱？

options.en: Forty dollars
Two-and-a-half dollars
A hundred dollars
Nothing; he loses

explain.en: Smiley says, 'I'll risk forty dollars that he can outjump any frog in Calaveras County,' and the feller 'put up his forty dollars along with Smiley's.'
explain.zh: 斯迈利说：'我愿拿四十美元打赌，它能跳赢卡拉维拉斯县任何一只青蛙。'陌生人'把他的四十美元和斯迈利的放在一起'。

q.en: How does the frame story (the narrator's visit) connect to Wheeler's embedded tale?
q.zh: 框架故事（叙述者的拜访）与惠勒嵌入的叙述之间有何关联？

options.en: The narrator expects information about Leonidas Smiley but gets an endless, irrelevant tale about Jim Smiley
options.zh: 叙述者本想打听利奥尼达斯·斯迈利，却听到一段没完没了、毫不相干、关于吉姆·斯迈利的故事

options.en: The narrator is actually Jim Smiley's brother
options.zh: 叙述者其实是吉姆·斯迈利的兄弟

options.en: Wheeler knows the narrator personally
options.zh: 惠勒与叙述者私交甚密

options.en: The story is entirely true history
options.zh: 这个故事完全是真实历史

explain.en: The narrator suspects Leonidas Smiley 'is a myth' and that asking would 'remind him of his infamous Jim Smiley' — and indeed Wheeler launches into the unrelated Jim Smiley story.
explain.zh: 叙述者怀疑利奥尼达斯·斯迈利'只是个虚构人物'，并料到一问就会让惠勒想起'他那臭名昭著的吉姆·斯迈利'——结果惠勒果然讲起了与主题无关的吉姆·斯迈利的故事。

q.en: Why does the narrator slip away before Wheeler finishes the story?
q.zh: 为什么叙述者在惠勒讲完之前就悄悄离开了？

options.en: He realizes the story won't yield information about Leonidas Smiley and loses interest
options.zh: 他意识到这故事根本不会提供关于利奥尼达斯·斯迈利的信息，于是失去了兴趣

options.en: He is afraid of Wheeler
He won a bet
He must catch a train

explain.en: 'I did not think that a continuation of the history of the enterprising vagabond Jim Smiley would be likely to afford me much information concerning the Rev. Leonidas W. Smiley, and so I started away.'
explain.zh: '我觉得再听那个爱折腾的流浪汉吉姆·斯迈利的故事，也不大可能让我得到关于利奥尼达斯·斯迈利牧师的什么消息，于是我就走了。'

prompt.en: The stranger wins by cheating. Have you ever seen or experienced a 'fair' competition that turned out to be rigged? What does this story suggest about who the real fool is?
prompt.zh: 陌生人靠作弊赢了。你有没有见过或经历过表面“公平“、实则被操纵的比赛？这个故事暗示谁才是真正的傻瓜？

en: Consider whether Smiley's own boastfulness and greed made him an easy target.
Think about how Twain uses a 'straight-faced' narrator to make the humor land.
zh: 想想斯迈利自己的自夸与贪心是否让他成了容易下手的目标。
思考吐温如何借助“面无表情“的叙述者让幽默生效。
